what_it_does: It is a channel-memory attachment metadata record that links attachment att-b098ea68 in the dragnet-development channel to board item item-45ed819d, recording only an added timestamp and no further content.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target is a board:// URI pointing to item-45ed819d
file body after frontmatter is empty
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme
The attachment_type board_items signals a board-item association
It records an exact ISO-8601 added timestamp
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Follows an id prefix convention att-*
Uses an ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone
References an external entity via a URI scheme
concerns: No payload or descriptive body beyond metadata
Authorship and reason for the attachment are not recorded
The referenced board item is not present in this file, so the link cannot be verified locally
notes: This file is a bare attachment pointer: it records that an attachment exists and which board item it targets, but carries no payload, title, or authorship.
questions: What content or artifact is being attached?

purpose_vs_actual: The file declares no explicit purpose, but its actual content is a typed cross-reference record, which is consistent with an attachment-to-board linking role.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register a dated, typed association between a channel-memory attachment and a specific board item so the relationship can be discovered and traversed.
novelty: The target uses a board:// URI scheme, which is less common than file paths or web URLs for local ledger references.
contribution: It contributes a persistent, timestamped, typed edge between a channel attachment and a board item, supporting relationship discovery without embedding the payload.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel-memory attachment record in the dragnet-development channel of type board_items pointing to board://item-45ed819d, created on 2026-06-24, with no body content.
intention_for_embedding: To record and expose a dated association between a channel attachment and a specific board item for traceability and navigation.
